name: Powell Library
insider_note: Main undergrad library. Night Powell opens overnight during finals week. The Rotunda and Rose Gilbert Reading Room are quietest.
description: The main undergraduate library on campus, Powell Library serves as a place for inquiry, creativity and community.
amenities: ['24 Hour Study Space', 'ADA Stations', 'Computer/Laptop Access', 'Laptop Lending', 'Print, Copy and Scan', 'Research Help']

name: Charles E. Young Research Library (YRL)
insider_note: Graduate-focused. Cafe 451 on 1st floor (named after Fahrenheit 451, written by Bradbury on this campus). Research Commons has high-tech collaborative spaces.
address:
280 Charles E. Young Drive, North
Los Angeles, CA 90095-1575
description: Named for UCLA's longest-serving chancellor, the Research Library houses research-level collections in a variety of humanities and social science fields.
amenities: ['ADA Stations', 'Cafe', 'Computer/Laptop Access', 'Laptop Lending', 'Locker', 'Print, Copy and Scan', 'Research Help']

name: Science and Engineering Library (SEL)
insider_note: Two locations: Boelter Hall 8th floor and Geology Building. The silent section at the far end of Boelter is legendary. Less crowded than Powell or YRL.
description: With its two locations, the Science and Engineering Library (SEL) houses research materials for physical sciences and engineering and features a variety of study spaces.
amenities: ['ADA Stations', 'Computer/Laptop Access', 'Laptop Lending', 'Print, Copy and Scan', 'Research Help']

name: Louise M. Darling Biomedical Library
insider_note: 24-hour reading room for health/life sciences grad students. 8 group study rooms on the 6th floor. Less foot traffic than north campus libraries.
address:
12-077 Center for the Health Sciences Box 951798
Los Angeles, CA 90095-1798
description: The Biomedical Library supports the teaching, research and patient care needs of the UCLA health and life science communities.
amenities: ['24 Hour Study Space', 'ADA Stations', 'Computer/Laptop Access', 'Laptop Lending', 'Print, Copy and Scan', 'Research Help']

name: Walter H. Rubsamen Music Library
insider_note: Very small and very quiet. Listening stations available. Rarely crowded — a hidden gem for focused solo study.
address:
1102 Schoenberg Music Building
Los Angeles, CA 90095-1490
description: The Music Library provides resources for music research, performance and study that span all music genres.

name: Richard C. Rudolph East Asian Library
insider_note: Tucked inside YRL. Very quiet. Good option when main YRL floors are packed.
address:
21617 Charles E. Young Research Library
Box 951575
Los Angeles, CA 90095-1575
description: The East Asian Library supports UCLA's teaching and research programs in East Asian studies.

name: Management Library
insider_note: Located in Cornell Hall at Anderson School of Management. Anderson Starbucks is nearby.

name: Arts Library
insider_note: Serves visual arts, architecture, and urban planning. Good natural light. Less crowded than big undergraduate libraries.
address:
1400 Public Affairs Building
Los Angeles, CA 90095-1392
description: The Arts Library is home to a comprehensive collection of materials in the fields of architecture, architectural history, art, art history, design, fashion and costume, film, television, photography, theater and allied disciplines.
amenities: ['Computer/Laptop Access', 'Laptop Lending', 'Print, Copy and Scan', 'Research Help']

name: Louise Kerckhoff Study Lounge
insider_note: One of the most beautiful study spots on campus. Get there early.
description: Originally the Women's Smoking Room, now a co-ed study lounge with comfortable couches, bright lighting, and stained glass bay windows.

name: Kerckhoff Second Floor Study Lounge
insider_note: Less known than the 3rd floor lounge — often easier to get a seat.
description: Smaller, cozier version of the 3rd floor lounge. Steps from Kerckhoff Coffeehouse.

name: Ackerman Union Open Study
insider_note: Hit or miss depending on room availability. Good for groups.
description: Any unreserved meeting room in Ackerman Union is available as open study space. Check the schedule posted next to each room entrance.
access: UCLA students, faculty, staff (unreserved rooms only)

name: Bruin Reflection Space
insider_note: Underrated for solo focused work. Very few people know about it.
description: Non-denominational space for contemplation, meditation, and prayer. Open to all Bruins. Extremely quiet.

name: Transfer Student Center Lounge
insider_note: Welcoming and rarely crowded. Good for commuters between classes.
description: Lounge inside the UCLA Transfer Student Center. Also supports commuter and non-traditional students.

name: Veteran Resource Center
insider_note: Tight-knit community. Great for veteran students.
description: Welcoming space for UCLA student veterans. Open for studying and community.

name: Kerckhoff Coffeehouse
insider_note: Iconic spot but tables fill fast. The outdoor patio is less crowded and great in good weather.
description: UCLA's first coffee house (est. 1975). Stunning gothic architecture. Sum One coffee, pastries, sandwiches, grilled cheese + soup. Tables with outlets along the pillars.
outlets_note: Outlets along the pillars — grab one early.

name: Cafe 451
insider_note: Best combo: coffee here then head upstairs to the quiet YRL stacks.
description: Named after Fahrenheit 451, written by Bradbury on this campus. Peet's coffee, pastries, sandwiches, salads, and sushi. Cozy study spot inside YRL.
outlets_note: Library wifi and outlets throughout YRL.

name: Jimmy's Coffee House
insider_note: Student favorite — less hectic than Kerckhoff. The outdoor tables behind Dodd Hall nearby are a hidden gem.
description: Named after Olympian Jimmy Lu Valle. Peet's coffee, espresso, matcha, pastries, sushi, light lunch. Indoor seating almost always less crowded than Kerckhoff.
outlets_note: Some outlets — try to snag a table near the wall.

name: The Study at Hedrick
insider_note: Arguably the best study cafe on campus. The noise-canceling fish bowl carrels are legendary.
description: European-style bakery cafe that doubles as a serious study space. Private study carrels, quiet reading rooms, discussion rooms with writable walls, fireplace, natural light, and abundant outlets.

name: Music Cafe
insider_note: Less known than Kerckhoff but equally charming. Next to the Inverted Fountain — a calming spot.
description: Modern cafe in the renovated Ostin Music Center near the Inverted Fountain. Sum One coffee, pastries, lunch items. Great atmosphere for music lovers and artists.
outlets_note: A few outlets — arrive early for a good seat.

name: Bruin Cafe
insider_note: Convenient for Hill residents. Outdoor patio is great on sunny LA days.
description: Casual dining cafe with indoor space and outdoor patio. Sandwiches, salads, soups, specialty coffees, teas, smoothies. Both indoor and outdoor areas are great study spots.

name: Anderson School Cafe (Starbucks)
insider_note: Great for a quick coffee before heading to the Management Library next door.
description: Starbucks inside Anderson School of Management. Accepts ASUCLA meal vouchers. Shaded outdoor seating nearby.

name: North Campus Student Center
insider_note: Rapid access to coffee, boba, and food makes this a great base for long study days.
description: Cluster of student-run restaurants near Rolfe Hall and the Robert Graham Sculpture Court. Includes coffee, boba, bowls, and quick bites. Large indoor and outdoor seating — popular study hub.
outlets_note: Outlets in seating areas — availability varies.

name: Franklin D. Murphy Sculpture Garden
insider_note: One of the most beautiful spots on campus. Especially magical during jacaranda season (April-May).
description: 5-acre outdoor sculpture museum with works by Calder, Rodin, Noguchi, Matisse, and Miro. Grassy hills, benches, shaded tables among sculptures and flowering trees. Jacaranda trees bloom brilliantly in spring.
food_nearby: Cafe 451 (YRL) and North Campus Student Center nearby

name: Kerckhoff Patio
insider_note: Go early — tables fill fast. The shaded umbrella tables are premium spots.
description: Outdoor patio directly outside Kerckhoff Hall. Tables shaded by umbrellas or trees plus open sunny spots. Steps from Kerckhoff Coffeehouse. Indoor/outdoor combo unbeatable on good weather days.

name: Inverted Fountain Area
insider_note: Underrated as a study spot. Most people walk past rather than stop. The shaded pine benches are extremely peaceful.
description: The iconic Inverted Fountain (1968) where water flows downward. Surrounded by tall pine trees and benches. The sound of flowing water creates a naturally calming study environment.

name: Lu Valle Commons Outdoor Area
insider_note: A genuinely hidden gem. The Dodd/law school courtyard is very secluded. Perfect for students with North Campus classes.
description: The private lawn and outdoor tables behind Dodd Hall next to Lu Valle Commons. The nearby courtyard between Dodd Hall and the law school is secluded and peaceful.
food_nearby: Jimmy's Coffee House at Lu Valle Commons
best_for: solo or pair study, reading between North Campus classes

name: Ackerman Union Outdoor Terrace
insider_note: Gets busy mid-day. Better in the morning or late afternoon.
description: Outdoor seating area on the Ackerman Union terrace overlooking Ackerman Square. Shaded seating with a view of central campus. Close to printing, food, and the UCLA Store.
food_nearby: Multiple ASUCLA options inside Ackerman Union
best_for: casual study, group meetups, between-class sessions

name: Bunche Hall Palm Court
insider_note: Most people walk right past it. Feels outdoors but protected from weather. One of campus's true hidden gems.
description: A hidden atrium inside Bunche Hall (social sciences building). Palm tree-filled internal courtyard spanning four floors. Quiet, sheltered from wind, largely unknown outside social science students.

name: Mathias Botanical Garden
insider_note: Not for laptop work — no outlets. Perfect for reading a textbook on a warm afternoon or a mental break during finals.
description: 7-acre botanical garden with plant specimens from around the world. A stream, tropical plants, towering old trees, and flowers. Free admission. Best for reading or unwinding during long study days.

name: Court of Sciences
insider_note: Best during off-peak hours. Limited shade — bring sunscreen for long sessions.
description: Open plaza surrounded by engineering and science buildings. Benches, tables, and open space used by STEM students between classes. Easy access to Boelter Hall, Young Hall, and the SEL.
food_nearby: Vending machines nearby; Bruin Cafe within walking distance
best_for: group study, collaborative work, between-class sessions for STEM students
